arguments.task: Open official Close pages needed to verify product description, developer API/authentication, pricing or plan/access information, rate limits, and whether an MCP server exists. Prefer first-party pages under close.com or official docs domains. Read the strongest canonical pages that load in a browser, and return concise extracted findings with the supporting URLs for: one-line product description; all documented API auth methods; API surface type and breadth; rate limit notes; pricing/access tier for API usage; and any official MCP/server references or lack thereof. Do not rely on search-result snippets.

input.thought: Start browser research on official Close sources.

API Authentication Methods**\\nClose supports two documented authentication methods:\\n- **API Keys**: Best for scripts and server-side integrations; used via HTTP Basic Auth (API Key as username, empty password).\\n- **OAuth 2.0**: Best for user-facing third-party integrations.\\n*Source: [developer.close.com/api/overview](https://developer.close.com/api/overview)*\\n\\n**3. raw_result.description: Sales CRM for calling, emailing, texting, automation, and action.
raw_result.auth_notes: Close documents OAuth 2.0 for third-party user-facing integrations and API key authentication via HTTP Basic Auth with the API key as the username and an empty password; no separate bearer token scheme was verified from first-party docs.
raw_result.gating_reason: Close pricing shows self-serve plans and states API access is included in all plans; the site also offers a free trial path.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API with broad CRM coverage including leads, contacts, opportunities, tasks, custom objects, activities such as calls/emails/SMS/meetings, webhooks, and event log capabilities.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Close provides an official MCP server at mcp.close.com with HTTP Streamable transport for AI agent access to Close tools.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Close documents bucket-based rate limits applied per endpoint group, per organization, and per API key, with rate-limit response headers and 429 responses on exhaustion; docs note a standard limit of 40 requests per 10 seconds with higher burst allowances on some plans/endpoints.
raw_result.notable_signals: Developer docs are public and detailed, API access is included across plans, and Close publishes an official MCP server in addition to its REST API.

result.description: Sales CRM for calling, emailing, texting, automation, and action.
result.auth_notes: Close documents OAuth 2.0 for third-party user-facing integrations and API key authentication via HTTP Basic Auth with the API key as the username and an empty password; no separate bearer token scheme was verified from first-party docs.
result.gating_reason: Close pricing shows self-serve plans and states API access is included in all plans; the site also offers a free trial path.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API with broad CRM coverage including leads, contacts, opportunities, tasks, custom objects, activities such as calls/emails/SMS/meetings, webhooks, and event log capabilities.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Close provides an official MCP server at mcp.close.com with HTTP Streamable transport for AI agent access to Close tools.

result.rate_limit_notes: Close documents bucket-based rate limits applied per endpoint group, per organization, and per API key, with rate-limit response headers and 429 responses on exhaustion; docs note a standard limit of 40 requests per 10 seconds with higher burst allowances on some plans/endpoints.
result.notable_signals: Developer docs are public and detailed, API access is included across plans, and Close publishes an official MCP server in addition to its REST API.
